summary:
- The Shine-Dalgarno sequence on mRNA pairs with the anti-Shine-Dalgarno site on 16S rRNA, enabling accurate ribosomal placement at the translation start site.  
- This mechanism ensures efficient and error-free translation initiation in bacteria by aligning the start codon precisely in the ribosome’s P-site.  

Claim: In prokaryotes, the Shine-Dalgarno sequence enables ribosome binding through complementary pairing with 16S rRNA.  
Evidence: - It bases-pairs with the anti-Shine-Dalgarno sequence on 16S rRNA to position the ribosome correctly over the start codon. - This interaction ensures high-fidelity translation initiation by accurately placing the start codon in the ribosome’s P-site.  
Answer: B
